mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p < 0.05, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0%, labor market slack triggers recession expectations that suppress corporate profit forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of months where UNRATE > 4.0, or signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.01 during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer prices creates stagflationary pressure that forces monetary tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint high readings in RSAFS and CPIAUCSL yield positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production signals strengthening real economic activity that boosts near-term corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, with mean forward return < 0.0% and t-stat > 1.96.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust labor demand that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no statistical relationship with 20-day forward returns with Rank IC < 0.01 across all market regimes over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.2 million units signals deteriorating construction demand and broader economic weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 50% of months when HOUST < 1200, or the signal out-of-sample Sharpe ratio remains below 0.2 over rolling 3-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of elevated federal funds rates and declining consumer prices confirms a successful disinflation trajectory that reduces recession risk, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high rates and falling inflation yields negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.015 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth indicates strengthening aggregate demand that lifts corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP acceleration fails to predict positive 60-day returns during early-cycle phases, with mean forward return <= 0.0% and statistical significance p > 0.10.
